entries.name: reduceBooleansWithAnd
entries.description: Reduces an array of booleans with the logical AND operation.

params.name: array
params.description: - Array of boolean values to reduce.

params.name: emptyArrayValue
params.description: - Value to return if the array is empty. If not provided and the array is empty, a TypeError will be thrown.

entries.returns: The result of ANDing all boolean values in the array.
entries.example:
reduceBooleansWithAnd([true, true, true]);   // true
reduceBooleansWithAnd([true, false, true]);   // false
reduceBooleansWithAnd([], true);              // true (emptyArrayValue)

entries.name: reduceBooleansWithOr
entries.description: Reduces an array of booleans with the logical OR operation.

params.name: array
params.description: - Array of boolean values to reduce.

params.name: emptyArrayValue
params.description: - Value to return if the array is empty. If not provided and the array is empty, a TypeError will be thrown.

entries.returns: The result of ORing all boolean values in the array.

entries.name: booleanFactory
entries.description: Creates a new BooleanFactory that generates random boolean values based on chance.

params.name: config
params.description: - Configuration for the boolean factory, including the chance of returning true.

entries.returns: A factory function (`BooleanFactory`) that generates random boolean values based on the configured chance.

entries.name: randomBoolean
entries.description: Returns a random boolean based on the specified chance.

params.name: chance
params.description: - Number between 0.0 and 100.0 representing the percentage chance of returning true (default: 50, i.e., 50%).

entries.returns: A random boolean value with the specified probability of being true.

entries.name: batch
entries.description: Splits the input array into batches of a maximum size. Each batch carries its zero-based index as `.i`.

params.name: input
params.description: - The array to split into batches.

params.name: batchSize
params.description: - Maximum number of items per batch.

entries.returns: An array of {@link IndexedBatch } arrays.

entries.name: batchCalc
entries.description: Calculates batch metrics (count, full batches, remainder) from a {@link BatchCount} configuration.

params.name: input
params.description: - The total items and items-per-batch configuration.

entries.returns: A {@link BatchCalc } with computed batch counts and remainder.

entries.name: restoreOrder
entries.signature: restoreOrder(orderKeys: K[], values: T[], { readKey, chooseRetainedValue = (values: T[]) => values[0], excludeNewItems = false }: RestoreOrderParams<T, K>): T[]
entries.description:
Reorders values to match a reference key ordering. Values not present in the order keys
are appended at the end (unless `excludeNewItems` is true). Duplicates are resolved via
the `chooseRetainedValue` function (defaults to keeping the first).

params.name: orderKeys
params.description: - Keys defining the desired order.

params.name: values
params.description: - Values to reorder.

params.name: { readKey, chooseRetainedValue = (values: T[]) => values[0], excludeNewItems = false }

entries.returns: The reordered values array.

entries.name: separateValues
entries.description: Separates values into included and excluded groups based on a decision function.

params.name: values
params.description: - Values to separate.

params.name: checkInclusion
params.description: - Returns `true` for values that should be included.

entries.returns: A {@link SeparateResult } with included and excluded arrays.

entries.name: decodeHashedValues
entries.signature: decodeHashedValues(hashedValues: string[], decodeValues: string[], hashFn: (value: string) => string): string[]
entries.description: Decodes a list of hashed string values using a provided list of potential original values and a hash function.

params.name: hashedValues
params.description: - An array of hashed strings to decode.

params.name: decodeValues
params.description: - An array of potential original string values.

params.name: hashFn
params.description: - A function that takes a string and returns its hashed representation.

entries.returns: An array of decoded strings. Values that cannot be decoded are filtered out.

entries.name: makeHashDecodeMap
entries.signature: makeHashDecodeMap(decodeValues: string[], hashFn: (value: string) => string): HashDecodeMap
entries.description:
Creates a `HashDecodeMap` from a list of potential original string values and a hash function.
The map's keys are the hashed versions of the `decodeValues`, and the values are the original `decodeValues`.

params.name: decodeValues
params.description: - An array of potential original string values.

params.name: hashFn
params.description: - A function that takes a string and returns its hashed representation.

entries.returns: A {@link HashDecodeMap } for decoding hashed values.

entries.name: iterate
entries.description: Iterates over each value in the array sequentially, awaiting each callback before proceeding to the next.

params.name: values
params.description: - Array of values to iterate over.

params.name: useFn
params.description: - Callback invoked for each value.

entries.returns: Promise<void>

entries.name: compareFnOrder
entries.description:
Convenience function that reverses the order of the sorted values if the order is specified descending.

The input comparison function must be in ascending order.

params.name: ascendingCompareFn
params.description: - a comparison function that sorts in ascending order

params.name: order
params.description: - the desired sort direction; defaults to 'asc'

entries.returns: the original function if ascending, or a reversed version if descending

entries.name: compareWithMappedValuesFunction
entries.signature: compareWithMappedValuesFunction(mapValue: MapFunction<T, V>, comparesFunction: SortCompareFunction<V>): SortCompareFunction<T>
entries.description:
Creates a {@link SortCompareFunction} that maps values to a different type before comparing.
Useful for sorting objects by a derived property.

params.name: mapValue
params.description: - Maps each value to the type used for comparison.

params.name: comparesFunction
params.description: - Compares the mapped values.

entries.returns: A sort comparison function for the original type.

entries.name: isObjectWithConstructor
entries.description:
Returns true if the input is a function-like value with a prototype and constructor (i.e., a class or named function declaration).
Returns false for arrow functions, class instances, plain objects, and primitives.

params.name: obj
params.description: - The value to check.

entries.returns: Whether the value is a function with a constructor.

entries.name: isClassLikeType
entries.description:
Returns true if the input is a class (requires `new` to instantiate). Distinguishes classes from regular functions
by checking that the prototype is non-writable.

params.name: obj
params.description: - The value to check.

entries.returns: Whether the value is a class type.

entries.name: getFunctionType
entries.description:
Determines the function type of the input value: `'class'`, `'function'`, or `'arrow'`.
Returns `null` if the input is not a function.

params.name: x
params.description: - The value to inspect.

entries.returns: The {@link FunctionType }, or `null` for non-functions.

entries.name: isNonClassFunction
entries.description: Returns true if the input is a function but not a class (i.e., a regular function or arrow function).

params.name: x
params.description: - The value to check.

entries.returns: Whether the value is a non-class function.

entries.name: arrayFactory
entries.description: Creates a new ArrayFactory that generates multiple values.

params.name: factory
params.description: - The factory function used to generate each item

entries.returns: A function that takes a count parameter and returns an array of generated items

entries.name: arrayInputFactory
entries.description: Creates an ArrayInputFactory that transforms input values into output values.

params.name: factory
params.description: - The factory function used to transform each input value

entries.returns: A function that takes an array of input values and returns an array of output values

entries.name: arrayDecisionFunction
entries.description:
Creates an {@link ArrayDecisionFunction} from a per-element predicate and a {@link SetIncludesMode}.

When mode is `'any'`, the resulting function returns `true` if at least one element satisfies the predicate.
When mode is `'all'`, it returns `true` only if every element satisfies the predicate.

params.name: decision
params.description: - Predicate used to test individual elements.

params.name: mode
params.description: - Whether all or any elements must satisfy the predicate.

entries.returns: A function that evaluates an array against the configured decision criteria.

entries.name: arrayDecision
entries.signature: arrayDecision(values: T[], decision: ArrayFindDecisionFunction<T>, mode: SetIncludesMode): boolean
entries.description: Convenience wrapper that creates and immediately invokes an {@link ArrayDecisionFunction}.

params.name: values
params.description: - Array to evaluate.

params.name: decision
params.description: - Predicate used to test individual elements.

params.name: mode
params.description: - Whether all or any elements must satisfy the predicate.

entries.returns: `true` if the array satisfies the decision criteria for the given mode.

entries.name: randomPickFactory
entries.description: Creates a {@link RandomPickFactory} from the input values.

params.name: values
params.description: - array of values to randomly pick from

entries.returns: a callable factory that returns a random value from the array on each invocation

entries.name: randomArrayIndex
entries.description: Returns a random index from the input array. Returns 0 if the array is empty.

params.name: values
params.description: - array to generate a random index for

entries.returns: a random valid index within the array, or 0 if the array is empty

entries.name: pickOneRandomly
entries.description: Picks a single item randomly from the input array.

params.name: values
params.description: - array to pick a random item from

entries.returns: a randomly selected item from the array

entries.name: keepValuesFromArray
entries.description: Returns items that exist in both arrays (intersection).

params.name: values
params.description: - The source array to filter.

params.name: secondArray
params.description: - The array of values to keep.

entries.returns: A new array containing only the values from `values` that also exist in `secondArray`.

entries.name: excludeValuesFromArray
entries.description: Returns items from the first array that do not exist in the second array (difference).

params.name: values
params.description: - The source array to filter.

params.name: secondArray
params.description: - The array of values to exclude.

entries.returns: A new array containing only the values from `values` that do not exist in `secondArray`.

entries.name: arrayContainsDuplicateValue
entries.description: Checks whether the given array contains any duplicate values.

params.name: values
params.description: - The array to check for duplicates.

entries.returns: `true` if the array contains at least one duplicate value, `false` otherwise.

entries.name: findIndexOfFirstDuplicateValue
entries.description: Finds the index of the first duplicate value in the given array.

params.name: values
params.description: - The array to search for duplicates.

entries.returns: The index of the first value that is a duplicate of an earlier value, or `-1` if no duplicates exist.

entries.name: convertMaybeToNonEmptyArray
entries.description: Converts the input value to an array containing itself, or returns itself if it is a non-empty array. Returns undefined if the input is nullish or results in an empty array.

params.name: arrayOrValue
params.description: - single value or array to convert

entries.returns: an array with at least one element, or undefined if the result would be empty

entries.name: convertMaybeToArray
entries.description: Converts the input value to an array containing itself, or returns itself if it is an array. Returns an empty array if the input is nullish.

params.name: arrayOrValue
params.description: - single value, array, or nullish value to convert

entries.returns: the input wrapped in an array, the input array itself, or an empty array if nullish

entries.name: convertToArray
entries.description: Converts the input value to an array containing itself, or returns itself if it is already an array.

params.name: arrayOrValue
params.description: - single value or array to convert

entries.returns: the input array unchanged, or a new single-element array wrapping the input value

entries.name: firstValue
entries.description: Returns the first value from the array, or the value itself if not an array.

params.name: input
params.description: - single value or array to retrieve from

entries.returns: the first element of the array, or the input value itself

entries.name: lastValue
entries.description: Returns the last value from the array, or the value itself if not an array.

params.name: input
params.description: - single value or array to retrieve from

entries.returns: the last element of the array, or the input value itself

entries.name: firstAndLastValue
entries.description:
Returns a tuple with the first and last value of the input.

If the input is not an array, returns that value as both the first and last value.

params.name: input
params.description: - single value or array to retrieve from

entries.returns: a two-element tuple of the first and last values

entries.name: valueAtIndex
entries.description: Returns the value at the given index from an array, or the value itself if not an array.

params.name: input
params.description: - single value or array to retrieve from

params.name: index
params.description: - zero-based index of the element to retrieve

entries.returns: the element at the specified index, or the input value itself if not an array

entries.name: concatArrays
entries.description: Concatenates the input arrays into a single array, filtering out nullish entries.

params.name: arrays
params.description: - arrays to concatenate; nullish entries are ignored

entries.returns: a single flattened array containing all elements from the non-nullish input arrays

entries.name: flattenArray
entries.description: Flattens a two-dimensional array into a single-dimensional array. Any null/undefined entries in the outer dimension are filtered out.

params.name: array
params.description: - two-dimensional array to flatten, may contain nullish entries

entries.returns: a single-dimensional array with all elements from the non-nullish inner arrays

entries.name: copyArray
entries.description: Creates a shallow copy of the input array. Returns an empty array if the input is nullish.

params.name: input
params.description: - array to copy, or nullish

entries.returns: a new array with the same elements, or an empty array if input is nullish

entries.name: mergeArrays
entries.description: Merges all input arrays into a single new array. Nullish entries are ignored.

params.name: arrays
params.description: - arrays to merge; nullish entries are skipped

entries.returns: a new array containing all elements from the provided arrays

entries.name: takeFront
entries.description: Copies/takes the elements from the front of the array up to the specified maximum.

params.name: values
params.description: - source array to take from

params.name: maxToTake
params.description: - maximum number of elements to take from the front

entries.returns: a new array containing at most maxToTake elements from the front

entries.name: splitFront
entries.description: Splits the array into two arrays, the first being the front of the array up to the maxToTake, and the second being the remaining values.

params.name: values
params.description: The array to split.

params.name: maxToTake
params.description: The maximum number of values to take from the front of the array.

entries.returns: The front and remaining values.

entries.name: takeLast
entries.signature: takeLast(values: T[], maxToTake: number, keepFromFront?: number): T[]
entries.description: Copies/takes as many elements as possible from the end.

params.name: values
params.description: Values to take from.

params.name: maxToTake
params.description: Max number of values to take from the end of the input array.

params.name: keepFromFront
params.description: Number of values to retain in the front of the array. These are not taken.

entries.returns: New array with the subset of taken values.

entries.name: countAllInNestedArray
entries.description: Counts the total number of elements across all inner arrays of a nested array.

params.name: array
params.description: - two-dimensional array whose elements are counted

entries.returns: the total number of elements across all inner arrays

entries.name: removeValuesAtIndexesFromArrayCopy
entries.description: Creates a copy of the array with the items at the specified indexes removed.

params.name: array
params.description: - source array to copy from

params.name: removeIndexes
params.description: - indexes of elements to exclude from the copy

entries.returns: a new array without the elements at the specified indexes

entries.name: concatArraysUnique
entries.description: Concatenates multiple arrays and returns only unique values.

params.name: arrays
params.description: - Arrays to concatenate. Null/undefined arrays are ignored.

entries.returns: Array containing only unique values from all input arrays.

entries.name: flattenArrayUnique
entries.description: Flattens a 2D array and returns only unique values.

params.name: array
params.description: - Two-dimensional array to flatten and deduplicate.

entries.returns: Array containing only unique values from the flattened input.

entries.name: unique
entries.signature: unique(values: T[], excludeInput?: FilterUniqueFunctionExcludeKeysInput<T, T>): T[]
entries.description: Filters the input values and only returns unique values.

params.name: values
params.description: - Array of primitive-key values to deduplicate.

params.name: excludeInput
params.description: - Optional keys or values to exclude from the result.

entries.returns: Array containing only unique values with exclusions removed.

entries.name: filterUniqueFunction
entries.signature: filterUniqueFunction(readKey: ReadKeyFunction<T, K>, additionalKeysInput?: FilterUniqueFunctionAdditionalKeysInput<T, K>): FilterUniqueFunction<T, K>
entries.description: Creates a {@link FilterUniqueFunction} that deduplicates items by their computed key.

params.name: readKey
params.description: - Function to extract a unique key from each item.

params.name: additionalKeysInput
params.description: - Optional keys or values to pre-seed as already seen, causing them to be excluded.

entries.returns: A reusable filter function that removes duplicate items from arrays.

entries.name: filterUniqueValues
entries.signature: filterUniqueValues(values: T[], readKey: ReadKeyFunction<T, K>, additionalKeys?: K[]): T[]
entries.description: Filters an array to contain only items with unique keys.

params.name: values
params.description: - Array of items to deduplicate.

params.name: readKey
params.description: - Function to extract a unique key from each item.

params.name: additionalKeys
params.description: - Optional keys to pre-seed as already seen, excluding matching items.

entries.returns: Array containing only the first occurrence of each uniquely-keyed item.

entries.name: isUniqueKeyedFunction
entries.description: Creates an {@link IsUniqueKeyedFunction} that checks whether all items in an array have unique keys.

params.name: readKey
params.description: - Function to extract a unique key from each item.

entries.returns: A decision function that returns true if all items have distinct keys.

entries.name: filterMaybeArrayValues
entries.description: Filters all maybe values from the input array. If a maybe value is input, returns an empty array.
entries.returns: An array containing only non-null and non-undefined values.

entries.name: filterEmptyArrayValues
entries.description: Filters all empty and maybe values from the input array. If a maybe value is input, returns an empty array.
entries.returns: An array containing only non-null, non-undefined, and non-empty values.

entries.name: memoizeAsyncValueCache
entries.description:
Wraps an inner {@link AsyncValueCache} with a single-load in-memory memoization layer.

The first {@link AsyncValueCache.load} call delegates to the inner cache and stores the
result; subsequent calls return the memoized value without touching the inner cache again.
{@link AsyncValueCache.update} writes through to the inner cache and refreshes the memo.
{@link AsyncValueCache.clear} clears both layers.

Note: the memoized value is per-process. Long-running processes will not observe writes
made by other processes to the inner backing once the memo is populated.

params.name: inner
params.description: - The backing cache to memoize. Reads are delegated once and cached; writes are forwarded through and refresh the memo.

entries.returns: An {@link AsyncValueCache } that proxies the inner cache with a single-load memoization layer.
entries.example:
const memo = memoizeAsyncValueCache(inMemoryAsyncValueCache<string>('initial'));
await memo.load();           // delegates to inner.load() once
await memo.load();           // returns memoized value without hitting inner
await memo.update('next');   // writes through to inner and refreshes memo

entries.name: memoizeAsyncKeyedValueCache
entries.description:
Wraps an inner {@link AsyncKeyedValueCache} with a single-load in-memory memoization layer
over the full record.

The first call to any read method ({@link AsyncKeyedValueCache.load} or {@link AsyncKeyedValueCache.get})
delegates to the inner cache and stores the loaded record; subsequent reads return entries
from the memoized record without re-hitting the inner cache. Writes ({@link AsyncKeyedValueCache.set} /
{@link AsyncKeyedValueCache.remove}) update the memoized record and write through to the inner cache.
{@link AsyncKeyedValueCache.clear} clears both layers.

Note: the memoized record is per-process. Long-running processes will not observe writes
made by other processes to the inner backing once the memo is populated.

params.name: inner
params.description: - The backing keyed cache to memoize. The full record is loaded once and cached; writes are forwarded through and applied to the memo.

entries.returns: An {@link AsyncKeyedValueCache } that proxies the inner cache with a record-level memoization layer.
entries.example:
const memo = memoizeAsyncKeyedValueCache(inMemoryAsyncKeyedValueCache<number>({ a: 1 }));
await memo.get('a');           // delegates to inner.load() once
await memo.get('a');           // returns memoized entry without hitting inner
await memo.set('b', 2);        // writes through to inner and updates memo

entries.name: readDomainsFromEmailAddresses
entries.description: Extracts unique domain names from a list of email addresses (case-insensitive).

params.name: addresses
params.description: - Array of email addresses to extract domains from

entries.returns: Array of unique lowercase domain strings

entries.name: readDomainFromEmailAddress
entries.description: Extracts the domain portion from a single email address.

params.name: address
params.description: - The email address to extract the domain from

entries.returns: The lowercase domain string

entries.name: readEmailDomainFromUrlOrEmailAddress
entries.description:
Reads a domain from various input formats including URLs, email addresses, and raw domains.

Supported formats:
- A url: www.test.com
- A url with the protocol (://): https://www.test.com
- An email address: test@test.com
- A domain: test.com

The "www." prefix is stripped from URL-style inputs since emails typically don't use it.

params.name: urlLikeInput
params.description: - A URL, email address, or domain string

entries.returns: The extracted domain

entries.name: convertParticipantToEmailParticipantString
entries.description:
Converts an EmailParticipant object to a formatted string representation.
The format is: "name<email>" or "<email>" if no name is provided.

params.name: participant
params.description: - The email participant to convert

entries.returns: A formatted string representation of the participant

entries.name: convertEmailParticipantStringToParticipant
entries.description:
Converts a formatted participant string into an EmailParticipant object.
Parses strings in the format "name<email>" or "<email>".

params.name: participantString
params.description: - The string to parse

entries.returns: An EmailParticipant object with the extracted name and email

entries.name: coerceToEmailParticipants
entries.signature: coerceToEmailParticipants({ participants = [], emails = [] }: { participants?: EmailParticipant[]; emails?: EmailAddress[] }): EmailParticipant[]
entries.description:
Combines an array of EmailParticipants with an array of email addresses.
Email addresses that don't already exist in the participants array are converted to EmailParticipant objects.
entries.returns: A combined array of EmailParticipant objects

entries.name: unixDateTimeSecondsNumberFromDateOrTimeNumber
entries.description: Converts a Date object or unix timestamp number to a unix timestamp number.

params.name: input
params.description: - Date object or unix timestamp number to convert

entries.returns: Unix timestamp number if input is valid, null/undefined if input is null/undefined

entries.name: unixDateTimeSecondsNumberForNow
entries.description: Gets the current time as a unix timestamp number.
entries.returns: Current time as unix timestamp number

entries.name: unixDateTimeSecondsNumberToDate
entries.description: Converts a unix timestamp number to a Date object.

params.name: dateTimeNumber
params.description: - Unix timestamp number to convert

entries.returns: Date object if timestamp is valid, null/undefined if timestamp is null/undefined

entries.name: expirationDetails
entries.description:
Returns an {@link ExpirationDetails} for the given input configuration.

Use this when you need to ask whether something has expired or what its expiration date is. See {@link ExpirationDetailsInput} for how the expiration date is resolved (`expires` → `expiresAt` → `expiresFromDate + expiresIn`).

Common patterns:
- **Direct expiration**: `expirationDetails({ expiresAt }).hasExpired()`
- **Wrap an existing object**: `expirationDetails({ expires: token }).hasExpired()`
- **TTL / throttle**: `expirationDetails({ expiresFromDate: lastRunAt, expiresIn: throttleMs })` — see {@link isThrottled}
- **Pre-emptive refresh**: `expirationDetails({ expiresFromDate: expiresAt, expiresIn: -bufferMs })` — treat as expired `bufferMs` before the real expiration, e.g. to refresh a token before it actually dies

params.name: input
params.description: - Configuration for calculating expiration

entries.returns: An ExpirationDetails object that can determine expiration state

entries.name: calculateExpirationDate
entries.description:
Convenience function for calculating and returning the expiration date given the input.
This is a shorthand for expirationDetails(input).getExpirationDate().

params.name: input
params.description: - Input configuration used to calculate the expiration date

entries.returns: The calculated expiration date, or null if no expiration is defined

entries.name: isExpired
entries.description: Convenience wrapper around {@link expirationDetails}().hasExpired() that treats null/undefined input or no expiration date as expired.

params.name: input
params.description: - Expiration configuration. Null/undefined is treated as expired.

params.name: now
params.description: - Optional override for the current time. Defaults to the current time. Apply any buffer (e.g. for clock skew or pre-emptive refresh) by shifting this value forward.

entries.returns: True when the input is null/undefined or its expiration date has passed; otherwise false.
entries.example:
isExpired(null); // true
isExpired({ expiresAt: pastDate }); // true
isExpired({ expiresAt: futureDate }); // false
isExpired({ expiresAt: futureDate }, addMilliseconds(new Date(), 60_000)); // true (when within buffer)

entries.name: isUnderThreshold
entries.signature: isUnderThreshold(threshold: Milliseconds, nextRunAt: Maybe<DateOrUnixDateTimeMillisecondsNumber>, now?: Maybe<Date>): boolean
entries.description:
Returns true if the threshold has not passed since the next run time, compared to now.

Used in cases where a date that was calculated using the "threshold" value is given and we want to check if we are still within that threshold.

Example:
- Should send a notification at max every 2 days. The threshold is 2 days in milliseconds, and "nextRunAt" is the previously calculated date that was originally "now" + "threshold".

params.name: threshold
params.description: The threshold time. Typically this is amount of time that was used to calculate the original "nextRunAt" time.

params.name: nextRunAt
params.description: Time the next run will occur. If null/undefined, then this function will return false.

params.name: now
params.description: Optional override for the current time. Defaults to the current time.

entries.returns: True if the threshold has not passed since the next run time, compared to now.

entries.name: isThrottled
entries.signature: isThrottled(throttleTime: Maybe<Milliseconds>, lastRunAt: Maybe<DateOrUnixDateTimeMillisecondsNumber>, now?: Maybe<Date>): boolean
entries.description:
Convenience function for quickly calculating throttling given a throttle time and last run time.

Returns true if the throttle time has not passed since the last run time, compared to now.
This is useful for rate limiting operations (e.g., "only allow this action once every X milliseconds").

params.name: throttleTime
params.description: - Minimum time in milliseconds that must pass between operations

params.name: lastRunAt
params.description: - Timestamp when the operation was last performed

params.name: now
params.description: - Optional override for the current time (defaults to the current time)

entries.returns: True if the operation should be throttled (not enough time has passed), false otherwise

entries.name: checkAtleastOneNotExpired
entries.description:
Returns true if at least one of the input ExpirationDetails has not expired.
Useful for checking if any items in a collection are still valid.

If the list is empty, returns false.

params.name: details
params.description: - Collection of ExpirationDetails to check

entries.returns: True if at least one item has not expired, false otherwise

entries.name: checkAnyHaveExpired
entries.description:
Returns true if any of the input ExpirationDetails have expired.
Useful for checking if any items in a collection need to be refreshed or removed.

If the list is empty, returns the value specified by defaultIfEmpty.

params.name: details
params.description: - Collection of ExpirationDetails to check

params.name: defaultIfEmpty
params.description: - Default value to return if the list is empty (defaults to true)

entries.returns: True if any item has expired, or the defaultIfEmpty value for an empty list

entries.name: isGetter
entries.description: Returns true if the input value is a non-class function (i.e., likely a Getter).

params.name: value
params.description: - The value to check

entries.returns: True if the value is a non-class function

entries.name: asGetter
entries.description: Wraps the input as a Getter function. If it's already a function, returns it directly.

params.name: input
params.description: - A value or getter function

entries.returns: A Getter function that returns the value

entries.name: objectCopyFactory
entries.description: Creates a factory that returns a shallow copy of the input value on each call.

params.name: value
params.description: - The object to copy

params.name: copyFunction
params.description: - Optional custom copy function (defaults to copyObject)

entries.returns: A factory that produces copies of the value

entries.name: iterableToArray
entries.description:
Converts an IterableOrValue to an array.

By default treats strings as a non-iterable value, using the string as a single value.

params.name: values
params.description: - The value or iterable to convert

params.name: treatStringAsIterable
params.description: - Whether to treat strings as iterable (defaults to false)

entries.returns: An array containing the value(s)

entries.name: iterableToSet
entries.description:
Converts an IterableOrValue to a Set.

By default treats strings as a non-iterable value, using the string as a single value.

params.name: values
params.description: - The value or iterable to convert

params.name: treatStringAsIterable
params.description: - Whether to treat strings as iterable (defaults to false)

entries.returns: A Set containing the value(s)

entries.name: isIterable
entries.signature: isIterable(values: unknown, treatStringAsIterable?: boolean): values is Iterable<T>
entries.description:
Type guard that returns true if the input is an Iterable.
By default, strings are not treated as iterable.

params.name: values
params.description: - The value to check

params.name: treatStringAsIterable
params.description: - Whether to treat strings as iterable (defaults to false)

entries.returns: True if the value is iterable

entries.name: isEmptyIterable
entries.description: Returns true if the iterable has no values.

params.name: values
params.description: - The iterable to check

entries.returns: True if the iterable is empty

entries.name: isInNumberBoundFunction
entries.description: Creates a function that checks whether a number falls within the specified inclusive bounds.

params.name: bounds
params.description: - The min/max bounds to test against

entries.returns: A function that returns `true` if the input number is within bounds

entries.name: wrapNumberFunction
entries.description:
Creates a function that wraps numbers around within the specified bounds, like modular arithmetic.

When `fencePosts` is true, wraps to the nearest "fence post" value, extending the wrap range by one in each direction.

params.name: wrapNumberFunctionConfig
params.description: - Configuration with min, max, and optional fence post behavior

entries.returns: A function that wraps input numbers into the bounded range

entries.name: boundNumberFunction
entries.description:
Creates a function that constrains numbers within bounds, either by clamping or wrapping.

When `wrap` is true, uses modular wrapping. Otherwise, clamps values to the min/max range.

params.name: boundNumberFunctionConfig
params.description: - Configuration with min, max, and optional wrap behavior

entries.returns: A function that bounds input numbers into the configured range

entries.name: boundNumber
entries.signature: boundNumber(input: number, min: T, max: T): T
entries.description: Clamps the input number between the min and max values (inclusive).

params.name: input
params.description: - Number to clamp

params.name: min
params.description: - Minimum allowed value

params.name: max
params.description: - Maximum allowed value

entries.returns: The clamped value

entries.name: percentNumberToDecimal
entries.description:
Converts a {@link PercentNumber} (e.g., 5 for 5%) to its decimal equivalent (e.g., 0.05).

Returns 0 for null/undefined input.

params.name: input
params.description: - A percent number value (e.g., 5 means 5%)

entries.returns: The decimal equivalent

entries.name: percentNumberFromDecimal
entries.description:
Converts a {@link PercentDecimal} (e.g., 0.05 for 5%) to its {@link PercentNumber} equivalent (e.g., 5).

Returns 0 for null/undefined input.

params.name: input
params.description: - A decimal percent value (e.g., 0.05 means 5%)

entries.returns: The percent number equivalent

entries.name: asNumber
entries.description:
Converts a number, string, or null/undefined value to a number.

Strings are parsed via `Number()`. Null/undefined returns 0.

params.name: input
params.description: - A number, number string, or null/undefined

entries.returns: The numeric value, or 0 for null/undefined

entries.name: isNumberDivisibleBy
entries.description:
Checks whether the input value is evenly divisible by the divisor.

Treats null/undefined as 0.

params.name: value
params.description: - The number to check

params.name: divisor
params.description: - The divisor to test against

entries.returns: `true` if the remainder is zero

entries.name: nearestDivisibleValues
entries.description: Finds the nearest values that are evenly divisible by the divisor, both above (ceil) and below (floor) the input value.

params.name: value
params.description: - The value to find divisible neighbors for

params.name: divisor
params.description: - The divisor to align to

entries.returns: Object with the input value, divisor, and the nearest ceil/floor divisible values

entries.name: isEvenNumber
entries.description: Checks whether the input is an even number.

params.name: value
params.description: - Number to test

entries.returns: `true` if even

entries.name: isOddNumber
entries.description: Checks whether the input is an odd number.

params.name: value
params.description: - Number to test

entries.returns: `true` if odd

entries.name: sumOfIntegersBetween
entries.description:
Computes the sum of all integers between two values, inclusive, using Gauss's formula.

The `from` value is floored and the `to` value is ceiled before computation.

params.name: from
params.description: - The starting value (floored to nearest integer)

params.name: to
params.description: - The ending value (ceiled to nearest integer)

entries.returns: Sum of all integers in the range

entries.name: minAndMaxNumber
entries.description: Finds the minimum and maximum values from an iterable of numbers.

params.name: values
params.description: - Iterable of numbers to examine

entries.returns: Object with `min` and `max` values

entries.name: getBaseLog
entries.description: Computes the logarithm of `y` with base `x`.

params.name: x
params.description: - The base of the logarithm

params.name: y
params.description: - The value to compute the logarithm of

entries.returns: The base-x logarithm of y

entries.name: randomNumberFactory
entries.description:
Creates a factory that generates random numbers within a configured range.

Accepts either a simple max number or a full config object with min, max, and rounding options.

params.name: maxOrArgs
params.description: - Maximum value (exclusive) or full configuration object

params.name: roundingInput
params.description: - Optional rounding mode override

entries.returns: A factory function that produces random numbers within the range

entries.name: randomNumber
entries.description: Generates a single random number using {@link randomNumberFactory}. Convenience function for one-off usage.

params.name: maxOrArgs
params.description: - Maximum value (exclusive) or full configuration object

params.name: roundingInput
params.description: - Optional rounding mode

entries.returns: A single random number

entries.name: roundingFunction
entries.description: Returns a rounding function for the specified rounding type.

params.name: type
params.description: - The rounding strategy: 'floor', 'ceil', 'round', or 'none'

entries.returns: The corresponding Math function, or an identity function for 'none'

entries.name: cutValueToPrecision
entries.description:
Truncates a number (or number string) to the specified decimal precision without rounding.

Accepts strings and null/undefined via {@link asNumber}.

params.name: input
params.description: - Number, number string, or null/undefined

params.name: precision
params.description: - Number of decimal places to retain

entries.returns: The truncated number value

entries.name: cutValueToInteger
entries.description: Truncates a value to an integer by cutting to zero decimal precision.

params.name: input
params.description: - Number, number string, or null/undefined

entries.returns: The truncated integer value

entries.name: objectIsEmpty
entries.description:
Recursively checks whether an object is "empty" — meaning it is null/undefined, has no keys,
or all of its values are themselves empty (recursively for nested objects, or falsy for primitives).

params.name: obj
params.description: - Object to check

entries.returns: `true` if the object is considered empty

entries.name: areEqualPOJOValues
entries.description:
Performs a deep equality comparison between two values.

Recursively compares arrays, objects, Maps, Sets, primitives, and Dates.

params.name: a
params.description: - First value to compare

params.name: b
params.description: - Second value to compare

entries.returns: `true` if the values are deeply equal

entries.name: flattenObject
entries.signature: flattenObject(input: Maybe<Record<string, unknown>>, config?: FlattenObjectConfig): Record<string, unknown>
entries.description:
Flattens a nested object into a single-level object with concatenated keys.

Recursively traverses plain objects and joins nested keys with a separator (default `'.'`).
Non-plain-object values (arrays, Dates, null, functions, etc.) are treated as leaf values and kept as-is.
Empty nested objects are omitted from the result (they produce no keys).
Circular references are detected and treated as leaf values to avoid infinite recursion.

params.name: input
params.description: - The object to flatten. If nullish, returns an empty object.

params.name: config
params.description: - Optional configuration for separator and max depth.

entries.returns: A new flat object with concatenated key paths.

entries.name: objectHasNoKeys
entries.description: Checks whether the object has no own enumerable keys.

params.name: obj
params.description: - Object to check

entries.returns: `true` if the object has zero keys

entries.name: mapToObject
entries.description: Converts a Map to a plain object by iterating entries and assigning key-value pairs.

params.name: map
params.description: - Map to convert

entries.returns: A plain object with the same key-value pairs

entries.name: copyObject
entries.description: Creates a shallow copy of an object using the spread operator.

params.name: input
params.description: - Object to copy

entries.returns: A new object with the same properties

entries.name: SLASH_PATH_SEPARATOR
entries.description: The forward-slash character used to separate parts of a slash path.

entries.name: SLASH_PATH_FILE_TYPE_SEPARATOR
entries.description: The dot character used to separate the file extension from the file name within a slash path.

entries.name: DEFAULT_SLASH_PATH_ILLEGAL_CHARACTERS
entries.description: The default set of characters that are considered illegal in a slash path.
entries.returns: string[]

entries.name: DEFAULT_SLASH_PATH_ILLEGAL_CHARACTER_REPLACEMENT
entries.description: Default replacement character for illegal characters.

entries.name: slashPathType
entries.description: Determines the type of a slash path string.

params.name: input
params.description: - The slash path to classify.

entries.returns: The path type classification.

entries.name: isSlashPathFile
entries.description: Type guard that checks if the input is a file path (typed or untyped).

params.name: input
params.description: - The string to check.

entries.returns: Whether the input is a file path.

entries.name: isSlashPathTypedFile
entries.description: Type guard that checks if the input is a typed file path (contains a file extension).

params.name: input
params.description: - The string to check.

entries.returns: Whether the input is a typed file path.

entries.name: isSlashPathFolder
entries.description: Type guard that checks if the input is a folder path (ends with a slash).

params.name: input
params.description: - The string to check.

entries.returns: Whether the input is a folder path.

entries.name: isValidSlashPath
entries.description: Type guard that checks if the input is a valid slash path (not 'invalid' type).

params.name: input
params.description: - The string to check.

entries.returns: Whether the input is a valid slash path.

entries.name: slashPathName
entries.description: Returns the last part of the slash path.

params.name: slashPath
params.description: - The path to extract the name from.

entries.returns: The last part of the path (file name or folder name).

entries.name: slashPathParts
entries.description: Returns each section of a SlashPath.

params.name: slashPath
params.description: - The path to split.

entries.returns: Array of non-empty path segments.

entries.name: slashPathStartTypeFactory
entries.description: Creates a function that enforces the specified start type on a slash path.

params.name: type
params.description: - The start type to enforce.

entries.returns: A function that transforms paths to the specified start type.

entries.name: toRelativeSlashPathStartType
entries.description: Converts a slash path to a relative path by removing all leading slashes.

params.name: input
params.description: - The slash path to convert.

entries.returns: A relative path without leading slashes.

entries.name: slashPathFolderFactory
entries.description: Creates a SlashPathFolderFactory.

params.name: config
params.description: Configuration options for the factory.

entries.returns: A SlashPathFolderFactory.

entries.name: slashPathFolder
entries.description:
Converts the input string to a valid slash path folder based on the configuration.

If the input is a file, the folder of the file is returned instead.

params.name: input
params.description: - the string path to convert to a folder path

params.name: config
params.description: - optional configuration controlling path type inference and invalid-path handling

entries.returns: a valid slash path folder string with a trailing slash, or an empty string for relative root

entries.name: toAbsoluteSlashPathStartType
entries.description: Converts a slash path to an absolute path by ensuring exactly one leading slash.

params.name: input
params.description: - The slash path to convert.

entries.returns: An absolute path starting with a single slash.

entries.name: fixMultiSlashesInSlashPath
entries.description: Replaces consecutive double slashes with single slashes.

params.name: input
params.description: - The slash path to fix.

entries.returns: The path with double slashes collapsed.

entries.name: replaceMultipleFilePathsInSlashPath
entries.description: Replaces consecutive double slashes with single slashes. Alias for {@link fixMultiSlashesInSlashPath}.

params.name: input
params.description: - The slash path to fix.

entries.returns: The path with double slashes collapsed.

entries.name: removeTrailingSlashes
entries.description: Removes all trailing slashes from a slash path.

params.name: input
params.description: - The slash path to trim.

entries.returns: The path without trailing slashes.

entries.name: removeTrailingFileTypeSeparators
entries.description: Removes all trailing dots from a slash path.

params.name: input
params.description: - The slash path to trim.

entries.returns: The path without trailing dots.

entries.name: addTrailingSlash
entries.description: Adds a trailing slash to the input if it does not already have one.

params.name: input
params.description: A slash path.

entries.returns: A slash path folder.

entries.name: replaceInvalidFilePathTypeSeparatorsInSlashPath
entries.description: Replaces all extra and invalidate FilePathTypeSeparator values from the SlashPath, returning a valid SlashPath.
entries.returns: SlashPath

entries.name: replaceInvalidFilePathTypeSeparatorsInSlashPathFunction
entries.description: Creates a function that replaces all extra and invalidate FilePathTypeSeparator values from the SlashPath, returning a valid SlashPath.
entries.returns: SlashPathFunction

entries.name: slashPathValidationFactory
entries.description: Creates a validation/fixup function for slash paths that replaces illegal characters and extra dots.

params.name: config
params.description: - Configuration for validation behavior.

entries.returns: A function that validates and fixes a slash path.

entries.name: slashPathFactory
entries.description: Creates a factory function that merges path segments together with optional base path, start type enforcement, and validation.

params.name: config
params.description: - Configuration for path generation.

entries.returns: A factory function that merges input paths into a single validated slash path.

entries.name: mergeSlashPaths
entries.description: Merges an array of path segments into a single slash path, filtering nullish values and collapsing double slashes.

params.name: paths
params.description: - Array of path segments to merge.

entries.returns: The merged slash path.

entries.name: slashPathInvalidError
entries.description: Creates an Error indicating that a slash path is invalid.
entries.returns: A new Error with a descriptive message.

entries.name: isolateSlashPath
entries.description: Splits the path and returns the items at the given ranges.

params.name: path
params.description: - The path to isolate parts from.

params.name: range
params.description: - Index range defining which path segments to extract.

entries.returns: A new slash path containing only the segments within the range.

entries.name: isolateSlashPathFunction
entries.description: Creates an IsolateSlashPathFunction.

params.name: config
params.description: - Configuration with range, optional start type, and file mode.

entries.returns: A function that isolates path segments within the configured range.

entries.name: slashPathDetails
entries.description: Returns the details of a path.

params.name: path
params.description: The path to get details for.

entries.returns: The details of the path.

entries.name: expandSlashPathPathMatcherPartToDecisionFunctions
entries.description: Expands the input matcher path into decision functions.

params.name: path
params.description: - Matcher path parts to expand into decision functions.

entries.returns: Array of decision functions for each path part.

entries.name: slashPathPathMatcherConfig
entries.description: Creates a SlashPathPathMatcherConfig from the input.

params.name: input
params.description: The configuration input.

entries.returns: The configuration.

entries.name: slashPathPathMatcher
entries.description: Creates a SlashPathPathMatcher.

params.name: input
params.description: - the matcher configuration, which may be a target path string, an array of path parts, or a full config object

entries.returns: The matcher.

entries.name: slashPathSubPathMatcher
entries.description: Creates a SlashPathSubPathMatcher.

params.name: config
params.description: The configuration for the matcher.

entries.returns: The matcher.

entries.name: isPromise
entries.signature: isPromise(obj: Promise<T> | S): obj is Promise<T>
entries.description: Checks whether the input value is a native Promise by testing for a `.then` method.

params.name: obj
params.description: - The value to test.

entries.returns: `true` if the value is a Promise, `false` otherwise.

entries.name: isPromiseLike
entries.signature: isPromiseLike(obj: PromiseLike<T> | S): obj is PromiseLike<T>
entries.description:
Checks whether the input value is PromiseLike (i.e., has a `.then` method), which
includes both native Promises and custom thenables.

params.name: obj
params.description: - The value to test.

entries.returns: `true` if the value is PromiseLike, `false` otherwise.

entries.name: poll
entries.signature: poll({ check, wait = 250, timesToGiveup = Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER }: PollConfig): Promise<void>
entries.description: Polls at a regular interval until a condition is met or the maximum number of attempts is reached.
entries.returns: A Promise that resolves when the check condition returns `true` or the max attempts are exhausted.

entries.name: runAsyncTaskForValue
entries.description: Runs a single async task and returns the resulting value. Always configured to throw on failure.

params.name: taskFn
params.description: - The async task to execute.

params.name: config
params.description: - Optional configuration for retries and retry behavior.

entries.returns: The value produced by the task, or undefined if the task produced no value.

entries.name: runAsyncTasksForValues
entries.signature: runAsyncTasksForValues(input: T[], taskFn: PerformAsyncTaskFn<T, K>, config?: RunAsyncTasksForValuesConfig<T>): Promise<K[]>
entries.description:
Runs an async task for each input value and returns an array of the resulting values.
Always configured to throw on failure.

params.name: input
params.description: - The array of input values to process.

params.name: taskFn
params.description: - The async task function to run for each input value.

params.name: config
params.description: - Optional configuration for parallelism and retries.

entries.returns: An array of results produced by the task function for each input.

entries.name: performAsyncTasks
entries.signature: performAsyncTasks(input: I[], taskFn: PerformAsyncTaskFn<I, O>, config?: PerformAsyncTasksConfig<I, K>): Promise<PerformAsyncTasksResult<I, O>>
entries.description:
Performs async tasks for each input value with configurable retry and parallelism behavior.
Useful for operations that may experience optimistic concurrency collisions.

params.name: input
params.description: - The array of input values to process.

params.name: taskFn
params.description: - The async function to execute for each input.

params.name: config
params.description: - Configuration for retries, parallelism, and error handling.

entries.returns: An aggregated result with succeeded/failed items and their outputs/errors.

entries.name: performAsyncTask
entries.description: Performs a single async task with configurable retry behavior and returns the result with success status.

params.name: taskFn
params.description: - The async task to execute.

params.name: config
params.description: - Optional configuration for retries and error handling.

entries.returns: A result object containing the value (if successful) and a success flag.

entries.name: asPromise
entries.description: Wraps the input in a resolved Promise if it is not already a Promise.

params.name: input
params.description: - A value or Promise to normalize into a Promise.

entries.returns: A Promise that resolves to the input value.

entries.name: asSet
entries.description: Converts an {@link IterableOrValue} into a Set. Strings are treated as single values rather than character iterables.

params.name: values
params.description: - The value or iterable to convert.

entries.returns: A new Set containing all values.

entries.name: addToSet
entries.description: Adds one or more values to the given set in place.

params.name: set
params.description: - The set to add values to.

params.name: values
params.description: - The value or iterable of values to add.

entries.returns: void

entries.name: toggleInSet
entries.description: Toggles values in the set in place: adds if absent, removes if present.

params.name: set
params.description: - The set to modify.

params.name: values
params.description: - The values to toggle.

entries.returns: void

entries.name: screamingSnakeToCamelCase
entries.description:
Pure string-case converters that do not pull in the
{@link change-case-all} dependency.

Use these for the common SCREAMING_SNAKE ↔ camelCase conversions
(constant-name ↔ symbol-name flips) without paying for the full
change-case bundle. For arbitrary case detection / round-tripping
across mixed inputs, prefer the dedicated package.

Converts a SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE string into camelCase.

Empty segments (e.g. leading underscores in `_USER_ID`) are skipped.

params.name: input
params.description: The SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE input.

entries.returns: The camelCase form.

entries.name: camelOrPascalToScreamingSnake
entries.description:
Converts a camelCase or PascalCase string into SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE.

Each upper-case character (other than the first) is preceded by an
underscore, then the whole result is upper-cased.

params.name: input
params.description: The camelCase / PascalCase input.

entries.returns: The SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE form.

entries.name: splitJoinRemainder
entries.signature: splitJoinRemainder(input: string, separator: string, limit: number): string[]
entries.description:
Splits a string like {@link String.prototype.split}, but joins overflow segments back together
instead of discarding them. Useful when you only want to split on the first N-1 occurrences.

params.name: input
params.description: - string to split

params.name: separator
params.description: - delimiter to split on

params.name: limit
params.description: - maximum number of resulting segments; overflow segments are rejoined with the separator

entries.returns: array of string segments, with length at most equal to limit

entries.name: capitalizeFirstLetter
entries.description: Capitalizes the first letter of the input string.

params.name: value
params.description: - string to capitalize

entries.returns: the input string with its first character uppercased

entries.name: lowercaseFirstLetter
entries.description: Lowercases the first letter of the input string.

params.name: value
params.description: - string to modify

entries.returns: the input string with its first character lowercased

entries.name: splitJoinNameString
entries.description:
Splits the input string into a first name and last name tuple using a space as the delimiter.
If the name contains more than one space, the remainder is treated as the last name.

params.name: input
params.description: - full name string to split

entries.returns: a tuple of [firstName, lastName], where lastName includes all text after the first space

entries.name: repeatString
entries.description: Creates a string that repeats the given string a specified number of times.

params.name: string
params.description: - the string to repeat

params.name: reapeat
params.description: - number of times to repeat the string

entries.returns: the repeated string concatenation

entries.name: cutStringFunction
entries.description: Creates a {@link CutStringFunction} that truncates strings exceeding the configured maximum length.

params.name: config
params.description: - configuration controlling max length and end text behavior

entries.returns: a reusable function that truncates input strings

entries.name: cutString
entries.signature: cutString(input: Maybe<string>, maxLength: number, endText?: Maybe<string>): Maybe<string>
entries.description: Truncates a string to the given maximum length, appending end text (defaults to "...") if truncated.

params.name: input
params.description: - the string to truncate, or null/undefined

params.name: maxLength
params.description: - maximum allowed length for the output string

params.name: endText
params.description: - text to append when truncated; defaults to "..."

entries.returns: the truncated string, or null/undefined if the input is null/undefined

entries.name: flattenWhitespace
entries.description:
Collapses consecutive whitespace characters (excluding newlines) into a single space, then trims.

Newlines are preserved.

params.name: input
params.description: - string to flatten

entries.returns: the string with collapsed whitespace

entries.name: simplifyWhitespace
entries.description: Reduces multiple consecutive newlines to a single newline and collapses multiple whitespace characters to a single space.

params.name: input
params.description: - string to simplify

entries.returns: the string with simplified whitespace and newlines

entries.name: expandFlattenTreeFunction
entries.signature: expandFlattenTreeFunction(expand: ExpandTreeFunction<T, N>, flatten: FlattenTreeFunction<N, V>): ExpandFlattenTreeFunction<T, V>
entries.description:
Creates an ExpandFlattenTreeFunction by composing an expansion function and a flattening function.

This higher-order function takes a function to expand an array of values `T` into a list of trees (`N[]` where `N` is a TreeNode)
and another function to flatten these trees into a single array of values `V`.

params.name: expand
params.description: An ExpandTreeFunction (values: T[]) => N[] that converts an array of T into an array of tree nodes N.

params.name: flatten
params.description: A FlattenTreeFunction (tree: N, array?: V[]) => V[] that flattens a tree of N nodes into an array of V values.

entries.returns: An ExpandFlattenTreeFunction (values: T[]) => V[] that performs the combined expansion and flattening.

entries.name: expandTreeFunction
entries.signature: expandTreeFunction(config: ExpandTree<T> | ExpandTreeWithNodeBuilder<T, N>): ExpandTreeFunction<T, N>
entries.description:
Implementation for expandTreeFunction. Creates a function that can expand a single value into a tree structure.

This factory function takes a configuration object that specifies how to retrieve children for any given value (`getChildren`)
and optionally, how to construct the nodes themselves (`makeNode`). If `makeNode` is not provided, a default node structure is used.
The returned function recursively builds a tree from a root value.

params.name: config
params.description: An ExpandTree<T> or ExpandTreeWithNodeBuilder<T, N> configuration object.

entries.returns: An ExpandTreeFunction<T, N> that takes a root value and returns its corresponding tree structure.

entries.name: expandTrees
entries.signature: expandTrees(values: T[], expandFn: ExpandTreeFunction<T, N>): N[]
entries.description:
Convenience function for expanding multiple root values into an array of trees.
Each value in the input array is treated as a root for a new tree.

params.name: values
params.description: An array of root values of type T to expand.

params.name: expandFn
params.description: An ExpandTreeFunction<T, N> used to expand each value into a tree.

entries.returns: An array of N, where each N is the root node of an expanded tree.

entries.name: ExploreTreeVisitNodeDecision
entries.signature: const ExploreTreeVisitNodeDecision: { readonly VISIT_ALL: 0; readonly VISIT_NODE_ONLY: 1; readonly VISIT_CHILDREN_ONLY: 2; readonly SKIP_ALL: 3; }
entries.description: Decides how to visit a node during tree exploration.
entries.returns: { readonly VISIT_ALL: 0; readonly VISIT_NODE_ONLY: 1; readonly VISIT_CHILDREN_ONLY: 2; readonly SKIP_ALL: 3; }

entries.name: exploreTreeFunction
entries.description:
Creates a reusable tree exploration function with configurable traversal, mapping, and filtering.

The returned function traverses one or more trees, applying a visit callback to each node.
By default uses depth-first traversal, identity mapping, and visits all nodes. All options
can be overridden per-call.

params.name: config
params.description: - Optional default configuration for mapping, filtering, and traversal strategy.

entries.returns: A reusable function that explores trees with the configured behavior.

entries.name: depthFirstExploreTreeTraversalFactoryFunction
entries.description:
Creates a depth-first traversal strategy for tree exploration.

Visits each node before its children (pre-order). This is the default traversal
strategy used by {@link exploreTreeFunction}.
entries.returns: A traversal factory that processes nodes in depth-first order.

entries.name: breadthFirstExploreTreeTraversalFactoryFunction
entries.description:
Creates a breadth-first traversal strategy for tree exploration.

Visits nodes level by level, processing all nodes at depth N before moving to depth N+1.
Uses an internal queue to defer child processing until the current level is complete.
entries.returns: A traversal factory that processes nodes in breadth-first order.

entries.name: FlattenTreeAddNodeDecision
entries.signature: const FlattenTreeAddNodeDecision: { readonly ADD_ALL: 0; readonly ADD_NODE_ONLY: 1; readonly ADD_CHILDREN_ONLY: 2; readonly SKIP_ALL: 3; }
entries.description: Decides how to add a node to the flattened array during flattening.
entries.returns: { readonly ADD_ALL: 0; readonly ADD_NODE_ONLY: 1; readonly ADD_CHILDREN_ONLY: 2; readonly SKIP_ALL: 3; }

entries.name: flattenTree
entries.description: Flattens a tree into an array containing all its nodes using depth-first traversal.

params.name: tree
params.description: - The root node to flatten.

params.name: addNodeFn
params.description: - Optional filter controlling which nodes and subtrees are included.

entries.returns: An array of all nodes in the tree that pass the filter.
entries.example:
const nodes = flattenTree(rootNode);
// Returns [root, child1, leaf1, leaf2, child2, leaf3, child3]

entries.name: flattenTreeToArray
entries.signature: flattenTreeToArray(tree: N, array: N[], addNodeFn?: Maybe<FlattenTreeAddNodeDecisionFunction<N>>): N[]
entries.description:
Flattens a tree and appends the resulting nodes to an existing array.

Useful for accumulating nodes from multiple trees into a single collection.

params.name: tree
params.description: - The root node to flatten.

params.name: array
params.description: - The target array to push flattened nodes into.

params.name: addNodeFn
params.description: - Optional filter controlling which nodes and subtrees are included.

entries.returns: The same array reference, now containing the appended nodes.

entries.name: flattenTreeToArrayFunction
entries.signature: flattenTreeToArrayFunction(mapNodeFnOrConfig?: FlattenTreeToArrayFunctionConfig<N, V> | ((node: N) => V), defaultAddNodeFn?: Maybe<FlattenTreeAddNodeDecisionFunction<N, V>>): FlattenTreeFunction<N, V>
entries.description:
Implementation for flattenTreeToArrayFunction.

Creates a reusable function that traverses trees and collects either the nodes themselves
or mapped values into an array. Supports both a simple function signature and a config object.

params.name: mapNodeFnOrConfig
params.description: - Optional mapping function or config object.

params.name: defaultAddNodeFn
params.description: - Optional default filter for node inclusion.

entries.returns: A reusable flattening function.

entries.name: build
entries.description:
Convenience function for imperatively constructing an object of a specific type by mutating a base object via a build function.

This is useful when building objects whose type is normally read-only, allowing incremental property assignment during construction.
entries.returns: the fully constructed object of type T

entries.name: safeEqualityComparatorFunction
entries.description:
Wraps an {@link EqualityComparatorFunction} to safely handle `Maybe` values.

The wrapped function delegates to the comparator only when both values are non-nullish.
When both are nullish, it uses strict equality (`===`), so `null === null` is `true`
but `null === undefined` is `false`.

params.name: compare
params.description: - the comparator to wrap

entries.returns: a new comparator that handles nullish values safely before delegating to the wrapped comparator
entries.example:
const safeCompare = safeEqualityComparatorFunction((a: number, b: number) => a === b);
safeCompare(1, 1);       // true
safeCompare(null, null);  // true
safeCompare(null, undefined); // false

entries.name: safeCompareEquality
entries.signature: safeCompareEquality(a: Maybe<T>, b: Maybe<T>, compare: EqualityComparatorFunction<T>): boolean
entries.description:
Convenience function that safely compares two `Maybe` values using the provided comparator.

Delegates to {@link safeEqualityComparatorFunction} internally, so nullish values are handled
without invoking the comparator.

params.name: a
params.description: - first value to compare

params.name: b
params.description: - second value to compare

params.name: compare
params.description: - the equality comparator for non-nullish values

entries.returns: `true` if the values are considered equal

entries.name: decisionFunction
entries.description:
Creates a {@link DecisionFunction} that always returns the given boolean, regardless of input.

Useful for providing a constant decision where a function is expected.

params.name: decision
params.description: - the constant boolean value to return

entries.returns: a decision function that always returns the given boolean
entries.example:
const alwaysTrue = decisionFunction(true);
alwaysTrue('anything'); // true

entries.name: asDecisionFunction
entries.signature: asDecisionFunction(valueOrFunction: Maybe<boolean | DecisionFunction<T>>, defaultIfUndefined?: boolean): DecisionFunction<T>
entries.description:
Normalizes a boolean value, a {@link DecisionFunction}, or undefined into a consistent {@link DecisionFunction}.

If the input is undefined, falls back to a constant function returning `defaultIfUndefined`.

params.name: valueOrFunction
params.description: - a boolean, decision function, or undefined

params.name: defaultIfUndefined
params.description: - fallback boolean when the input is nullish (defaults to true)

entries.returns: a {@link DecisionFunction } derived from the input

entries.name: isEqualContext
entries.description:
Creates an {@link IsEqualContext} that captures a reference value and uses the provided comparator
to check whether subsequent values are equal to it.

params.name: contextValue
params.description: - the reference value to compare against

params.name: fn
params.description: - the equality comparator

entries.returns: a function that checks whether a given value equals the captured reference

entries.name: areEqualContext
entries.description:
Creates an {@link AreEqualContext} that checks whether a single value or all values in an iterable
are equal to the captured reference value.

Returns `true` only if every value in the input matches the context value according to the comparator.

params.name: contextValue
params.description: - the reference value to compare against

params.name: fn
params.description: - the equality comparator

entries.returns: a function that checks whether all input values equal the captured reference

entries.name: allObjectsAreEqual
entries.description:
Returns `true` if all values in the input are equal according to the provided comparator.

Empty iterables and single-value inputs return `true` by default, since there is nothing to contradict equality.
Uses the first value as the reference and checks all remaining values against it.

params.name: values
params.description: - the values to compare

params.name: fn
params.description: - the equality comparator

entries.returns: `true` if all values are equal to each other, or if fewer than two values are provided

entries.name: mapMaybeFunction
entries.description:
Wraps a MapFunction so that null/undefined inputs are passed through without invoking the map,
avoiding errors on nullable values.

params.name: mapFunction
params.description: - function to apply only when the input is defined

entries.returns: a new function that short-circuits on null/undefined inputs

entries.name: mapArrayFunction
entries.description: Lifts a per-element MapFunction into one that operates on arrays, applying the mapping to each element.

params.name: mapFunction
params.description: - per-element transformation

entries.returns: a function that maps entire arrays

entries.name: MAP_IDENTITY
entries.description:
The canonical identity MapFunction. Returns its input unchanged.

Used as a sentinel value so that {@link chainMapSameFunctions} and other combinators can detect
and skip no-op mappings for efficiency.
entries.returns: the same input value, unmodified

entries.name: chainMapSameFunctions
entries.description:
Chains together multiple MapSameFunctions into a single pipeline executed left-to-right.
Null/undefined entries and identity functions are automatically removed for efficiency.
Returns the identity function if no meaningful functions remain.

params.name: input
params.description: - one or more optional same-type map functions to chain

entries.returns: a single composed function that runs all provided functions in order

entries.name: hasNonNullValue
entries.description: Type guard that returns `true` if the value is not `null` or `undefined`.

params.name: value
params.description: - the value to check

entries.returns: `true` if the value is not `null` or `undefined`

entries.name: hasValueOrNotEmpty
entries.description:
Type guard that checks whether the input has a meaningful value.

Returns `false` for empty iterables (arrays, Sets, Maps), empty strings, and nullish values.
Non-iterable objects (e.g. `{}`) are considered non-empty by this function;
use {@link hasValueOrNotEmptyObject} to also reject empty objects.

NaN has undefined behavior.

params.name: value
params.description: - the value to check

entries.returns: `true` if the value is non-nullish and not empty

entries.name: hasValueOrNotEmptyObject
entries.description:
Type guard that checks whether the input has a meaningful value, including checking for empty objects.

Returns `false` for empty iterables, empty strings, objects with no own keys (`{}`), and nullish values.
This is stricter than {@link hasValueOrNotEmpty}, which considers `{}` as having a value.

NaN has undefined behavior.

params.name: value
params.description: - the value to check

entries.returns: `true` if the value is non-nullish, non-empty, and not an empty object

entries.name: isNotNullOrEmptyString
entries.signature: isNotNullOrEmptyString(value: Maybe<MaybeNot | '' | T>): value is MaybeSo<T>
entries.description:
Type guard that returns `true` if the input is not nullish and not an empty string.

Useful for filtering out both nullish values and empty strings in a single check.

params.name: value
params.description: - the value to check

entries.returns: `true` if the value is not nullish and not an empty string

entries.name: isMaybeNot
entries.description: Type guard that returns `true` if the input is `null` or `undefined`.

params.name: value
params.description: - the value to check

entries.returns: `true` if the value is `null` or `undefined`

entries.name: isMaybeSo
entries.description:
Type guard that returns `true` if the input is neither `null` nor `undefined`.

Equivalent to {@link hasNonNullValue} but with the `MaybeSo` narrowing type.

params.name: value
params.description: - the value to check

entries.returns: `true` if the value is neither `null` nor `undefined`

entries.name: isDefinedAndNotFalse
entries.description: Returns `true` if the input is not `null`, `undefined`, or `false`.

params.name: value
params.description: - the value to check

entries.returns: `true` if the value is not `null`, `undefined`, or `false`

entries.name: isSameNonNullValue
entries.signature: isSameNonNullValue(a: Maybe<T>, b: Maybe<T>): a is NonNullable<T>
entries.description: Returns `true` if both inputs are non-nullish and strictly equal (`===`).

params.name: a
params.description: - first value

params.name: b
params.description: - second value

entries.returns: `true` if both values are non-nullish and strictly equal

entries.name: valuesAreBothNullishOrEquivalent
entries.description:
Returns `true` if both inputs are nullish (using loose equality `==`) or are strictly the same value.

This means `null` and `undefined` are considered equivalent to each other, but `false` and `null` are not.

params.name: a
params.description: - first value

params.name: b
params.description: - second value

entries.returns: `true` if both are nullish or both are the same value

entries.name: updateMaybeValue
entries.description:
Merges two `Maybe` values using `undefined` as the "no change" sentinel.

- If `b` is `undefined`, returns `a` (no update).
- If `b` is `null`, returns `null` (explicit clear).
- If `b` is defined, returns `b` (new value).

params.name: a
params.description: - the current value

params.name: b
params.description: - the update value

entries.returns: `a` if `b` is undefined, otherwise `b`

entries.name: modifier
entries.description: Creates a {@link Modifier} with the given key and modify function.

params.name: key
params.description: - unique identifier for the modifier

params.name: modify
params.description: - function that mutates the target value

entries.returns: a new {@link Modifier } pairing the key with the modify function
